methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Pivotal is a growth-stage Transportation company founded by Marcus Leng and backed by Page. The valuation universe for Transportation includes five identifiable private peers—Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, Harbinger, and REGENT Craft—with no public comparables available. The sector spans diverse subsegments, limiting direct peer alignment.

Peer-median triangulation is not applicable here. Of the five Transportation peers identified, no same-stage companies exist, only one adjacent-stage peer is available, and none fall into a similar valuation tier. This fragmentation falls short of the minimum three-datapoint threshold required for a reliable median-based valuation. Attempting an all-sector median would introduce material distortion given the heterogeneity of Transportation subsegments and the absence of public market reference points.

Without sufficient peer density or public market anchors, the traditional comparable-company method cannot be reliably applied to Pivotal. A defensible valuation would require alternative approaches—discounted cash flow analysis with sensitivity testing around Transportation sector unit economics, precedent transaction analysis if transaction data exists for peer financings, or structured scenario modeling around Pivotal's specific operational milestones and capital efficiency. The key uncertainty remains the lack of narrow-band peer data; any valuation range derived here should be explicitly qualified by this methodological constraint and stress-tested against sector-wide assumptions rather than company-specific comparables.
